name: Bluefin Tostada de Fuego
source: Inspired by Pujol, Chef’s Table at Brooklyn Fare, and KOL London
chef: Inspired by Enrique Olvera, César Ramirez & Santiago Lastra
description: A world-class modern Mexican starter featuring cured bluefin tuna on a black masa tostada with burnt avocado mousse, Oscietra caviar, smoked habanero oil, compressed strawberry and tomatillo pearls, hoja santa oil, and mezcal vapor.
keywords: Mexican fine dining, tuna tostada, Michelin starter, modernist Mexican cuisine, bluefin tuna, caviar tostada, mezcal smoke

winePairings.name: Mexican Chenin Blanc
winePairings.notes: Bright acidity and minerality complement the tuna and smoke.

winePairings.name: Champagne Blanc de Blancs
winePairings.notes: Crisp texture enhances the caviar and tostada crunch.

winePairings.name: Clarified Mezcal Martini
winePairings.notes: Smoky and clean, pairing naturally with the mezcal vapor.

steps.instruction: Dice the bluefin tuna into precise cubes and season lightly with white soy, yuzu juice, lime zest, and sea salt.
Fold finger lime pearls into the tuna and refrigerate for 10 minutes.
Mix blue corn masa with squid ink and water until smooth.
Press masa into ultra-thin rounds and cook lightly on cast iron.
Flash fry the tostadas in duck fat until blistered and crisp.
Char avocados directly over flame until blackened and smoky.
Blend avocado flesh with crème fraîche, charred jalapeño, lime juice, and smoked salt into a silky mousse.
Smoke habaneros over hardwood embers and blend with grapeseed oil and garlic confit.
Vacuum seal strawberries and tomatillos with champagne vinegar and coriander seed to compress and pickle them.
Bake chicken skin between sheet trays until deeply crisp, then pulverize with Tajín into chicharrón dust.
Blend hoja santa leaves with warm oil and strain until vivid green.
Arrange the black tostada off-center on a matte plate and pipe burnt avocado mousse asymmetrically.
Top with cured bluefin tuna and a quenelle of Oscietra caviar.
Add compressed strawberry and tomatillo pieces around the plate.
Dot smoked habanero oil and dust lightly with chicken skin chicharrón powder.
Finish with micro cilantro, edible flowers, hoja santa oil, and serve beneath a mezcal smoke cloche tableside.
Char avocados directly over open flame.
Blend avocado flesh with crème fraîche, jalapeño, lime juice, and smoked salt.
Process in Pacojet for ultra-smooth texture if available.
Smoke habaneros over hardwood embers.
Blend with warm oil and garlic confit.
Infuse gently for 1 hour and strain.
